title: Airbus space business takes advantage of growing demand for satellite systems
summary: Airbus's space business has rebounded in time to capitalize on increasing demand for satellite systems, particularly from Europe. The company is pursuing a joint venture to further leverage this growth.

title: China’s Shijian-31 satellite is sweeping GEO using a unique orbit
summary: China's Shijian-31 satellite, launched in June, is operating in a unique orbit that provides a distinctive view of the geostationary belt. This classified satellite's orbit is considered creative and offers strategic advantages.

title: FCC approves sweeping space reforms while clearing more C-band for 5G
summary: The FCC approved significant space industry reforms and allocated additional C-band spectrum for a 5G auction next year. The changes aim to accelerate the commercial space economy through a new licensing overhaul.

title: Vast establishes division for national security projects
summary: Vast has created a new division focused on national security space projects. This move aligns with the company's expansion beyond commercial space stations and spacecraft.

title: The strategic competition with China: winning the multi-domain race
summary: The article discusses the strategic competition with China, emphasizing the need to win the multi-domain race in military innovation. It highlights how historical military advancements have reshaped warfare and rendered past strategies obsolete.

title: Relativity Space to expand Terran R production in Florida
summary: Relativity Space has partnered with Space Florida to establish manufacturing and test facilities for its Terran R rocket on Florida’s Space Coast. This expansion aims to boost the company's production capabilities for the next-generation rocket.

title: Artemis 2 astronaut advocates for equatorial lunar landings
summary: The pilot of NASA's Artemis 2 mission suggests prioritizing human landings in the equatorial regions of the Moon for a safer and more manageable return, rather than targeting the challenging south pole.

title: Gravity-1 sea launch off Shanghai puts 9 satellites into orbit
summary: Orienspace successfully launched its third Gravity-1 solid rocket, deploying nine satellites into orbit from a sea launch off Shanghai. The 30-meter-long rocket marks another milestone for China's commercial space sector.

title: Zenno Astronautics to relocate to the United States
summary: Zenno Astronautics, a New Zealand startup specializing in superconducting magnets for spacecraft applications, will relocate its headquarters to the United States. The move aims to leverage the U.S. space industry ecosystem and regulatory environment.

title: SpaceX launches Northrop mission to extend the life of aging satellites
summary: SpaceX launched a Northrop Grumman mission to extend the life of aging satellites by installing propulsion pods. The DARPA-backed vehicle will service three commercial satellites in geostationary orbit.

title: 'Star Trek: Strange New Worlds'' season 4 opener is a pulpy sci-fi joy packed with dinosaurs, martians, and a 5th planet where the asteroid belt should be
summary: The season 4 premiere of 'Star Trek: Strange New Worlds' features dinosaurs, Martians, and a fifth planet in the asteroid belt, blending pulpy sci-fi adventure with themes of human survival. The episode sets a high bar for the series' signature blend of action and storytelling.

title: Should Artemis IV astronauts actually land near the moon's south pole? Artemis II pilot says the lunar equator might be better
summary: NASA's Artemis program has targeted the moon's south pole for its first crewed landing, but Artemis II astronaut Victor Glover suggests the lunar equator could be a safer and easier alternative.

title: The James Webb Space Telescope's disappearing 'Little Red Dots' may lead to another cosmic puzzle
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope's 'Little Red Dots' may disappear completely, presenting a new cosmic puzzle similar to the evolution of dinosaurs into birds. This discovery challenges current understanding of these distant celestial objects.

title: China's record-breaking Gravity-1 rocket launches 9 satellites from a ship at sea (video)
summary: China's Gravity-1 rocket, developed by Orienspace, successfully launched nine satellites into orbit from a ship at sea. This mission marked the third flight of the world's most powerful solid rocket.

title: After nearly 30 years, NASA realized this near-Earth asteroid is actually a comet. The discovery may help us defend the planet some day
summary: NASA discovered that a near-Earth asteroid is actually a comet after nearly 30 years. This finding could aid in identifying more hidden comets and enhancing planetary defense.

title: NASA's Roman Space Telescope could reveal black holes ripping up stars. It's set to launch Aug. 30
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, launching on August 30, may reveal black holes tearing apart stars. It could uncover hidden black holes even during cosmic noon.

title: If space war erupts, 'none of us will be able to avoid the war zone,' warns outgoing Space Force chief in final public speech
summary: The outgoing head of the U.S. Space Force warned in his final speech that a space war would affect everyone, as 'none of us will be able to avoid the war zone.' The statement highlights the potential global impact of conflict extending into space.

title: 'Terminator 2: Judgment Day' is time-travelling back into theaters next month for a 35th anniversary re-release
summary: The classic film 'Terminator 2: Judgment Day' will return to theaters for a 35th anniversary re-release next month. The event celebrates the movie's legacy with a nostalgic summer 1991 theme.

title: Astronomers may have discovered the 1st moon outside of our solar system — or is it something weirder?
summary: Astronomers may have discovered the first exomoon outside our solar system, but its nature remains uncertain. The observation could challenge existing definitions of planets and moons.

title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
summary: NASA will host a media tour and ribbon-cutting ceremony for its new Flight Dynamics Research Facility wind tunnel at Langley Research Center in Virginia. This is the agency's first new wind tunnel in over 40 years.

title: Robotic Servicing Mission Launches with NASA Support
summary: A robotic servicing mission, featuring the Mission Robotic Vehicle (MRV) with NASA's RSGS payload, launched on July 21 aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket to perform in-space spacecraft servicing in geosynchronous Earth orbit.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Chris Williams, Crewmates Return
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams and two Roscosmos cosmonauts are returning to Earth after a 241-day mission aboard the International Space Station. Their Soyuz MS-28 spacecraft is scheduled to undock and land on July 26.

title: A New Look – and Sound – for Messier 94
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory revealed a new image and sound representation of the spiral galaxy Messier 94, highlighting its starburst ring where new stars are forming. The galaxy's unique oval-shaped structure and active star formation were showcased in the June 30, 2026 release.

title: Mapping Io’s Hidden Heat With NASA’s Juno
summary: NASA's Juno spacecraft used its Microwave Radiometer (MWR) instrument to map areas of Jupiter's moon Io during two close flybys in December 2023. The data collected primarily focused on the northern hemisphere during Perijove 57 and Perijove 58.

title: NASA’s Juno Peers Beneath Io’s Surface
summary: NASA's Juno spacecraft has used its Microwave Radiometer to detect heat rising from beneath the surface of Jupiter's moon Io. The data reveals insights into the moon's subsurface structure and volcanic activity.

title: NASA’s Juno Takes Temperature of Jupiter’s Fiery Moon Io
summary: NASA's Juno mission measured the subsurface temperature of Jupiter's moon Io, revealing significant heating beneath its surface. The data, collected during two close flybys, highlights Io's extreme volcanic activity.

title: NASA Joins Genesis Mission to Accelerate AI-Driven Discovery
summary: NASA has joined the Genesis Mission, a national initiative aimed at accelerating AI-driven scientific and engineering breakthroughs. The mission was established via an executive order signed by President Donald J. Trump on November 24, 2025.

title: A Week of Smoky Skies Across North America
summary: Wildland fires in July 2026 emitted brown carbon, significantly degrading air quality across parts of the U.S. and Canada over a week. The event highlights the impact of wildfires on atmospheric conditions and public health.

title: Starship Flight 13 heads into second launch attempt
summary: SpaceX is preparing for a second attempt to launch Starship Flight 13 after a scrub last week. The mission aims to advance the development of the Starship rocket system.

title: Soyuz MS-29 crew arrives at ISS, U.S. EVA completes Canadarm2 repairs
summary: The Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft successfully docked at the International Space Station (ISS), bringing three new crew members. A U.S. spacewalk also completed repairs on the Canadarm2 robotic arm.

title: Path to Starship Flight 13 requires additional Booster 20 test
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Starship Flight 13, targeting a launch no earlier than July 23, but requires an additional test of Booster 20. The delay highlights ongoing technical challenges in the Starship program.

title: Falcon 9 to launch MRV-1 robotic servicing spacecraft for Northrop Grumman
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is scheduled to launch the MRV-1 robotic servicing spacecraft for Northrop Grumman. This mission aims to demonstrate in-space servicing capabilities.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Starship, and Chinese rockets scheduled to launch
summary: SpaceX is preparing for a new attempt at launching Starship Flight 13 after the previous attempt was aborted. Additionally, Falcon 9 and Chinese rockets are also scheduled for launches.

title: Making Progress: Relativity’s Terran R, Rocket Lab’s Neutron, and PLD Space’s Miura 5
summary: Relativity Space, Rocket Lab, and PLD Space are making progress on their respective launch vehicles: Terran R, Neutron, and Miura 5. While Starship's test program has dominated recent attention, these companies continue advancing their medium-to-heavy lift rocket developments.

title: China’s first recovered booster returns to port as LandSpace aims for first land recovery
summary: China achieved a milestone with the first successful recovery of a booster, and LandSpace is now aiming for the first land recovery of a booster.

title: Soyuz MS-29 launches new crew to ISS from Baikonur
summary: Soyuz MS-29 successfully launched a new crew to the International Space Station (ISS) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome on July 14. This marks another mission supporting the ongoing operations and research aboard the ISS.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Soyuz is set to launch a crew to the International Space Station (ISS), while SpaceX's Starship is preparing for its 13th flight test. Seven launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. This marks a key milestone ahead of its planned launch.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's significant milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: The silicon brains steering next-generation antennas
summary: The article discusses advancements in silicon-based technology for next-generation antennas. This innovation is expected to enhance communication and radar systems in the space industry.

title: Plato's electronics ready for space
summary: The European Space Agency's PLATO spacecraft has completed key electromagnetic compatibility tests in the Maxwell Test Chamber, confirming its electronics are fit for space. The mission is scheduled to launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in March 2027.

title: Test firing at brand new green chemical propulsion lab
summary: A new green chemical propulsion lab conducted its first test firing. This marks a significant step in developing environmentally friendly propulsion technologies.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a breakthrough in robotic arm technology for space applications. It highlights the versatility and potential applications of a new robotic arm system.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports European space science activities by involving research institutes.

title: NASA Puts $2B Price Tag on Nuclear Mars Mission
summary: NASA has estimated a $2 billion cost for the Space Reactor-1 Freedom, a fission-powered spacecraft intended for Mars missions. This marks the first interplanetary spacecraft powered by nuclear fission.

title: Intel Authorization Act Proposes Limiting Commercial EO Access
summary: The Intel Authorization Act seeks to restrict commercial Earth observation (EO) satellite operators from sharing data with the public, framing EO data as a privilege rather than a right. This move aims to increase government control over commercial EO access.

title: The UK Scraps the Department in Charge of UKSA
summary: The UK has decided to abolish the department overseeing the UK Space Agency (UKSA) during the Farnborough Air Show. This move comes as the country highlights its aerospace achievements under new Prime Minister Andy Burnham.

title: The UK and Space Florida Launch Joint Fund
summary: The UK and Space Florida have launched a joint fund to support projects with partners from both regions. This initiative aims to foster collaboration between entities in the UK and Florida.

title: Breaking Down the $686B Space Economy
summary: The global space economy reached a record $686 billion in 2025, as reported by the Space Foundation. This growth highlights the expanding commercial and technological advancements in the space sector.

title: FCC Eyes Wi-Fi and Bluetooth Bands for Satellites
summary: The FCC is considering a proposal to permit unlicensed wireless devices to communicate directly with satellites using Wi-Fi and Bluetooth bands. This move aims to expand satellite connectivity options for consumer devices.

title: deltaVision Raises €10.2M to Build In-Orbit Refueling Tech
summary: deltaVision has raised €10.2 million to develop in-orbit refueling technology. The funds will be used to expand manufacturing capacity and research new capabilities.

title: UK Commits £62M into Domestic Space Capabilities
summary: The UK government announced a £62 million ($83.3 million) investment to enhance domestic satellite communications and space technologies. This funding aims to bolster the UK's space capabilities.

title: Skyroot Aerospace Reaches Orbit with Vikram-1 Rocket
summary: Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched its Vikram-1 rocket, becoming the first private Indian company to reach orbit. The mission took off from India's Satish Dhawan Space Centre.

title: DoD Adds $11.4B to NatSec Launch Contracts
summary: The U.S. Department of Defense increased its national security space launch contract ceiling by $11.4 billion, raising the total from $5.6 billion to $17 billion. This expansion more than triples the pool of NSSL launch contracts.

title: FCC Approves a New C-Band Auction
summary: The FCC approved a new C-band auction to clear 160 megahertz of the upper C-band spectrum from satellite operators. This auction aims to build on previous C-band spectrum allocation efforts.

title: Lite Coms Wins $22M Contract to Support Operational Readiness
summary: Lite Coms secured a $22 million contract from an unnamed government and defense customer to provide satellite communications for operational readiness. The company serves as an integrator and manufacturer in the space industry.

title: FCC Overhauls Satellite Licensing, Adopts ‘Assembly Line’ Approach
summary: The FCC approved a major overhaul of its satellite and earth station licensing process, introducing a streamlined 'licensing assembly line' approach. The new system aims to expedite approvals and improve efficiency in the space industry.

title: Iridium Reports 4% Revenue Growth in Q2 Amid Rocket Lab Acquisition Deal
summary: Iridium reported a 4% revenue growth in Q2 alongside subscriber increases, as the company progresses through an acquisition deal with Rocket Lab. The satellite operator's financial performance highlights growth amid the ongoing merger process.

title: Contrivian Completes Merger With Big Network to Fuel Orchestration Service
summary: Contrivian has finalized its merger with Big Network to create a vertically integrated, multi-modal connectivity platform. The deal aims to enhance orchestration services in the space industry.

title: SpaceX Launches Northrop Grumman’s First Mission Robotic Vehicle
summary: SpaceX successfully launched Northrop Grumman's first Mission Robotic Vehicle (MRV-MEP) to orbit, which aims to provide on-orbit refueling and satellite life extension services. This marks a significant step in satellite servicing and space logistics.

title: Arianespace Sets Date for Fourth Ariane 6 Launch This Year
summary: Arianespace has scheduled the fourth Ariane 6 launch of the year for August 27, aiming to deploy the second Meteosat Third Generation Imager satellite, MTG-I2.

title: Poland Commits Nearly $750M to Europe’s IRIS² Constellation
summary: Poland has committed 656 million euros ($748 million) to Europe's IRIS² satellite constellation, covering nearly 10% of the project's funding. The agreement was signed in Warsaw.

title: UK Government to Invest Further Millions to Boost Space Sector
summary: The UK government plans to invest an additional £62 million ($83.4 million) to strengthen the country's space sector, focusing on new satellite communications systems.

title: Skyroot Aerospace Makes History for India With First Launch Success
summary: Skyroot Aerospace successfully achieved India's first private orbital launch with its Vikram-1 rocket. This historic mission marks a significant milestone for the country's growing space industry.
